title: ligularia hookeri f. angustifolium
intro: Ligularia hookeri f. angustifolium, also known as Japanese ligularia, is a flowering plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its large, yellow daisy-like flowers and broad, pointed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from arthritis, reduction of fever, and support for the immune system, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Ligularia hookeri f. angustifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
